danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite variants with mutated/deformed appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Post-apocalyptic threat; consequence of nuclear war world-state

danger_signals.visual_form: Uniformed/armored enemy sprites in castle environments
Health meter displayed on status bar during boss encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent major threat; escalated danger level

danger_signals.visual_form: Gothic fortress environments with multiple hazards
Baphomet statue with cross-to-swastika transformation detail
danger_signals.meaning: Dark ritual threat; supernatural danger from Ruth's cult activities

safety_signals.meaning: Victory point; safe haven for jewel restoration
Player support asset; cooperative gameplay mechanic reducing isolation danger
Progress checkpoints; reduced penalty for failure (meta-level safety)

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Altar of Peace; post-apocalyptic rebuild aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Jewels of corresponding colors; castle architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Uniformed soldiers per duke; boss character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Eight distinct castle environments (Spain, Egypt, Arabia, Italy, India, Africa, Germany stages)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Baphomet statue with occult markings (cross/swastika); demonic artifacts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: House of Ruth final castle; treasure-filled hidden chambers with golden statue

faction_indicators.faction: College of Arms / MI5 (Japanese version only)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Baronet Sir Julian James Bond identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruins and rebuilt structures; castle environments; nuclear wasteland visual aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mutant enemies roam; dukes hoard power; Great King banished to wastelands

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Late 19th-century architectural style; ancient excavation sites; demonic temple interiors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ruth's cult performs dark rituals; artifacts stolen; murders committed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight geographically distinct castles each housing a colored jewel; different regional aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dukes control territories independently; no unified government; each boss vulnerable only to matching-color sword

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Threat of world destruction / Nuclear danger
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jewels glow with mysterious power; wasteland environments; mutant creature designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest urgency; need to recover all 8 Eyes and return to Altar before catastrophe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Statue's cross pattern transforms to swastika; eyes open with 'crooked light'; golden surface tarnished
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dark ritual nearing completion; Bond fails to prevent supernatural threat

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight castles in Spain, Egypt, Arabia, Italy, India, Africa, Germany stages communicate global political division and scope of duke conspiracy
Red Sword defeats Red Jewel boss; Black Sword defeats Black Jewel boss. Visual color coding on both items and status bar telegraphs optimal progression path through cryptic clue hints
Each duke boss visually embodies their regional identity and power; boss defeat grants thematic sword that matches their jewel color

Japanese version shows collected scrolls as graphic elements; US version leaves blanks until translation accommodated. Scrolls contain logical puzzle about jewel placement order (no adjacent colors, specific end positions)
Baphomet statue described as originally golden but stained copper/lead by repeated blood rituals; visual degradation tells story of occult abuse without explicit exposition
Japanese version labels protagonist 'Bond'; US version 'Orin'. Regional storytelling variance communicates cultural adaptation of lore (baronet vs. falconer archetype)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Infiltrates castles; defeats soldiers and mutants; collects color-matched swords progressively
Second player controls; increases combat capability and puzzle-solving ability
Vulnerable only to matching-color sword; defeat yields that sword for next encounter
Faster movement in Special/Final stages (US version); spawn rate increases as player progresses
Commands cultists; accumulates treasures; attempts Baphomet awakening; final defeat leaves statue partially awakened (ominous ending)
Cannot act; depends on Orin/Bond to retrieve jewels and restore order

notes: 8 Eyes exhibits strong regional narrative divergence: the English post-apocalyptic version emphasizes nuclear war recovery and political theft, while the Japanese version frames the story as occult horror with demonic resurrection threat. Visual manifestations align with these themes — wasteland aesthetics vs. gothic supernatural. The jewel-sword color-matching system is the core mechanical manifestation of world-state communication, where visual color coding directly determines puzzle solutions and optimal play order. The Baphomet statue ending (Japanese version) uses visual degradation and symbol transformation (cross→swastika) as ominous storytelling without explicit text. Regional UI differences (status bar hero name, scoring vs. item extensions) subtly reinforce localized narrative flavor.
